reasoning: As of March 1, 2026, 11 of the 12 appropriations bills for FY2026 have been passed as full-year funding. However, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) remains a sticking point due to partisan disagreements over immigration enforcement, ICE, and CBP. The House passed a full-year DHS bill (H.R. 7147) on February 19, but this bill faces a steep climb in the Senate, which previously stripped DHS out of a larger package on January 30 and passed a temporary CR instead. Given the high polarization around DHS funding under the Trump administration and a divided or tightly-margin Senate, negotiations are highly likely to necessitate another stopgap CR extending through or past March 15, rather than achieving a full-year consensus in the next 14 days. The community forecast of 95% on February 19 strongly aligns with this expectation that Congress will still be governing DHS via temporary funding measures. Because the criteria specify resolving YES if Congress fails to complete a "complete full-year FY 2026 appropriations package", a CR covering even just DHS qualifies. I place the probability at 92%, leaving a small chance that a swift compromise is reached by mid-March.
